correctionContext.title: Creation correction: FAC-1
correctionContext.body:
Action: split
Original seed: FAC-1 Bundled Antarctic monolith discovery
Original wording: Researchers find passages into miles-deep monolithic architecture with strange remains and language-like traces while U.S. officials try to block foreign access.
Rationale: The passage discovery, artificial architecture, remains/language evidence, and foreign-access conflict need independently rejectable proposed facts.
Creation correction remains proposed-only; Admission owns first canon standing, severity, gate results, and admission outcomes.

kernelSections.heading: World premise
kernelSections.body: In an alternate 1950s, Antarctic researchers uncover access shafts into miles-deep monolithic architecture while the U.S. tries to block foreign access.

kernelSections.heading: Core promise
kernelSections.body: Every discovery creates awe, logistical strain, institutional fear, and contested access claims.

kernelSections.heading: Starting scale
kernelSections.body: One Antarctic field station, one ice tunnel, and the first diplomatic containment perimeter.

kernelSections.heading: Genre, tone, and consequence-mode commitments
kernelSections.body: Cold War scientific containment with material-institutional consequence pressure and restrained awe.

kernelSections.heading: Foundational facts
kernelSections.body: The first shafts are real; the architecture predates known human presence; evidence is fragmentary enough that interpretation remains dangerous.

kernelSections.heading: Primary pressures and initial domains
kernelSections.body: Field logistics, scientific publication, military secrecy, Soviet/Allied access, polar survival, and interpretation of remains.

handoff.granularityRationale: This deliberately parked seed is too bundled so the post-park critique has to split discovery, artificiality, remains, and access politics.

doctrineAtPointOfUse: Decision: Split broad steward material into smaller seed facts that can be independently rejected.
Operative rule: Decompose until each seed can be rejected without destroying its siblings, then park it as proposed for Admission.
Why the method asks: Creation finds workable pressure seeds; Admission owns first canon standing.
What good material looks like: Good seeds have a title, prose body, explicit truth layer, proposed canon status, and a granularity rationale or confirmation.
